title: pilosella kalksburgensis subsp. charadraea
intro: Pilosella kalksburgensis subsp. charadraea is a rare and endangered plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae), typically found in alpine and subalpine regions. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits associated with Pilosella kalksburgensis subsp. charadraea include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and potentially exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are usually made from the leaves and flowers, and can be consumed as teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
